senses.gloss: used in reference to animate beings that are perceived as capable of thinking such as humans and angels

etymology.text: Derived from the active participle of عَقَلَ (ʕaqala, “to be endowed with reason, to have intelligence, to be conscious, to realize”).

body.text: : (Msb:) the latter is an epithet in which the quality of a subst. predominates; (TA;) and signifies a man's
who league together to defend one another,
consisting of the relations on the father's side,
for him who has been slain unintentionally:

(S, O, TA:) it was decided by the Prophet that it was to be paid in three years, to the heirs of the person slain: (TA:) they look to the offender's brothers on the father's side, who, if they take it upon them, pay it in three years: if they do not take it upon them, the debt is transferred to the sons [meaning all the male descendants] of his grandfather; and in default of their doing so, to those of his father's grandfather; and in default of their doing so, to those of his grandfather's grandfather; and so on: it is not transferred from any one of these classes unless they are unable [to pay it]: and such as are enrolled in a register [of soldiers or pensioners or any corporation] are alike in respect of the bloodwit: (IAth, TA:) or, accord. to the people of El-'Irák, it means the
]: (S, O:) or it is applied to the
persons of the register which was that of the slayer; who derive their subsistence-money,
allowances, from the revenues of a particular register:
(Mgh:) Ahmad Ibn-Hambal is related to have said to Is-hák Ibn-Mansoor, it is applied to the
) [of the slayer]; but that they bear responsibility [only] in proportion to their ability; and that if there is no

, it [i. e. the bloodwit] is not to be from the property of the offender; but Is-hák says that in this case it is to be from the treasury of the state, the bloodwit not being [in any case] made a thing of no account: (TA:) the pl. of
being]; (S, O, Msb, K;) and so ↓
, (S, O, K,) or this latter has an intensive signification [i. e.
&c.]: (TA: [see an ex. in a saying cited voce
:]) the former is expl. by some as applied to a man
restrains, and turns back, his soul from its inclinations,
(TA:) and it is likewise applied to a woman, as also
, (Msb, K,) this latter pl. sometimes used; and the pl. fem. is
is also applied to a mountaingoat, as an epithet, signifying
That protects himself in his mountain from the hunter:
is said by Freytag to be used in the Deewán of Jereer.] And it is [also] a name for
(K;) because it renders itself inaccessible in a high mountain. (S, O, K. *) ―         -b3-  And
